title: Traveling with Tinnitus Air Pressure and Altitude
phrases: "We are not our thoughts, but we are responsible for our relationship to them."
"The nervous system doesn't respond to what you believe. It responds to what it senses."
"Sit with it long enough and even the worst feeling reveals its edges."
"The most sophisticated defense mechanism is the one that looks like wisdom."
interjections: And this is the part nobody talks about.
researchers: Sam Harris (secular meditation, neuroscience of consciousness)
existingBody:
The diagnosis was the easy part.

title: Exercise Guidelines for People with Tinnitus
phrases: "The nervous system doesn't respond to what you believe. It responds to what it senses."
"You don't arrive at peace. You stop walking away from it."
"What we call 'the present moment' is not a place you go. It's the only place you've ever been."
"What we call 'stuck' is usually the body doing exactly what it was designed to do under conditions that no longer exist."
"The most important things in life cannot be understood - only experienced."
interjections: I get it. Really, I do.
researchers: Bessel van der Kolk (body keeps the score, trauma and the body)
existingBody:
I've sat across from someone saying exactly what you're feeling right now.

title: Swimming and Water Sports with Tinnitus Considerations
phrases: "Most people don't fear change. They fear the gap between who they were and who they haven't become yet."
"The gap between stimulus and response is where your entire life lives."
"The self you're trying to improve is the same self doing the improving. Notice the circularity."
"What we call 'stuck' is usually the body doing exactly what it was designed to do under conditions that no longer exist."
interjections: Think about that for a second.

title: Concerts and Live Music with Tinnitus a Practical Guide
phrases: "Trauma reorganizes perception. Recovery reorganizes it again, but this time with your participation."
"The mind is not the enemy. The identification with it is."
"Not every insight requires action. Some just need to be witnessed."
"Every moment of genuine attention is a small act of liberation."

title: How to Handle Tinnitus Setbacks Without Spiraling
phrases: "The paradox of acceptance is that nothing changes until you stop demanding that it does."
"Your nervous system doesn't care about your philosophy. It cares about what happened at three years old."
"Most of what passes for healing is just rearranging the furniture in a burning house."
"You are not a problem to be solved. You are a process to be witnessed."
interjections: Think about that for a second.
And this is the part nobody talks about.
researchers: Bessel van der Kolk (body keeps the score, trauma and the body)
